title: Verify Whether Direct RMS Or External API Actions May Have Bypassed OptiSweep Tracking
summary: Use this source-based reference check when reviewing an issue to determine whether support or troubleshooting activity may have bypassed the normal OptiSweep software flow through direct RMS or external API interaction. The source warns that bypassing OptiSweep can leave the software unaware of what happened and create out-of-sync conditions.
when_to_use: Use when investigating missing visibility, unexplained state differences, or suspected support actions involving RMS, Geek Plus, REST API, or other external API interaction outside the normal OptiSweep software flow.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as authorization to perform direct RMS or external API actions.
Do not use this runbook as a recovery procedure to resynchronize OptiSweep after bypass activity.
Do not use this runbook to execute unsupported troubleshooting steps not explicitly provided by the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review reported actions, determine whether direct API or RMS interaction may have bypassed OptiSweep, and document the synchronization risk described by the source.

safety_notes: The source explicitly warns against bypassing the OptiSweep software flow.
Direct API activity may leave OptiSweep without knowledge of the transaction and create an out-of-sync condition.
This source does not provide a recovery method after bypass activity.
access_or_tools_needed: Incident details or operator/support report describing what actions were taken
Awareness of the documented OptiSweep-to-RMS integration boundary

system_preconditions: A reported problem, support action, or troubleshooting history is available for review.
external_references: RMS and OptiSweep communicate through APIs including REST API references

visual_references.description: Training video frame covering RMS overview and discussion of API communication between RMS and OptiSweep, including the warning about bypassing OptiSweep and causing systems to get out of sync.

steps.title: Confirm whether direct RMS or API interaction was attempted
steps.purpose: Establish whether troubleshooting or support activity included actions outside the normal OptiSweep software flow.
steps.instruction: Review the incident details or support report and confirm whether anyone attempted direct interaction with Geek Plus, RMS, REST API, or other external APIs outside the normal OptiSweep software flow.
steps.expected_result: A clear yes/no determination is made on whether direct external interaction was attempted.
steps.healthy_condition: No direct RMS or external API action outside OptiSweep is reported.
steps.failure_condition: Direct RMS or external API interaction outside OptiSweep is reported or cannot be ruled out.

actions.label: Review reported actions
actions.exact_action: Review the problem details and confirm whether any direct interaction with Geek Plus or RMS APIs was attempted outside the normal OptiSweep software flow.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: RMS overview context and transcript references to API communication between RMS and OptiSweep.

stop_or_escalate_if: Direct API use is suspected and system state is unclear.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Communication between RMS and what? And the OptiSweep it's API APIs. You mean REST API? Yes, REST API.

source_evidence.summary: Communication between RMS and OptiSweep is described as API-based, with REST API explicitly mentioned.

steps.title: Identify whether the action bypassed the OptiSweep service
steps.purpose: Determine whether the reported action was intended to bypass OptiSweep rather than use the supported software path.
steps.instruction: Identify whether the reported action was intended to bypass the OptiSweep service or directly trigger behavior through external APIs instead of going through the OptiSweep software flow.
steps.expected_result: The action is classified as either within the OptiSweep flow or a bypass of the OptiSweep service.
steps.healthy_condition: The reported action stayed within the OptiSweep software flow.
steps.failure_condition: The reported action bypassed the OptiSweep service or directly used external APIs.

actions.label: Classify bypass intent
actions.exact_action: Identify whether the reported action was intended to bypass the OptiSweep service or directly trigger behavior through external APIs.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript context describing RMS access and API communication, used to interpret whether the reported action was outside OptiSweep.

stop_or_escalate_if: The reported action appears to have bypassed the OptiSweep service.
It is not possible to determine whether the action used the supported OptiSweep path.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you have a problem and you want to bypass the OptiSweep service...

source_evidence.summary: The segment warns that bypassing OptiSweep software and using external APIs directly can leave OptiSweep unaware of the transaction and create out-of-sync conditions.

steps.title: Compare the action against the source warning
steps.purpose: Apply the source guidance to determine whether the reported action could explain missing visibility or synchronization issues.
steps.instruction: Compare the reported action against the source warning that bypassing OptiSweep can leave the software unaware of what happened and can cause systems to get out of sync.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether the source warning plausibly explains the observed issue.
steps.healthy_condition: The reported action does not conflict with the source warning, or no bypass occurred.
steps.failure_condition: The reported action matches the bypass scenario described by the source and may explain missing visibility or synchronization issues.

actions.label: Apply source warning
actions.exact_action: Compare the reported action against the source warning that OptiSweep may not know what happened if software is bypassed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Warning context in the transcript about bypassing OptiSweep and causing out-of-sync conditions.

stop_or_escalate_if: The source warning matches the reported action and current system state is unclear.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: You can't bypass our software or else we get out of sync...
If you have a problem and you want to bypass the OptiSweep service... the OptiSweep software doesn't know what happened. It has no knowledge of the database of that transaction.

steps.title: Record the synchronization risk
steps.purpose: Document the support interpretation that direct API activity may have left OptiSweep without transaction awareness.
steps.instruction: Record that suspected or confirmed direct API activity may have left OptiSweep without knowledge of the transaction and may have created an out-of-sync condition.
steps.expected_result: The case record reflects the source-supported explanation for missing visibility or synchronization issues.
steps.healthy_condition: The issue record clearly documents whether bypass activity is suspected and why it matters.
steps.failure_condition: The issue record does not capture the possibility that OptiSweep lacks knowledge of the transaction.

actions.label: Document sync risk
actions.exact_action: Record that direct API activity may leave OptiSweep without knowledge of the transaction and may create an out-of-sync condition.

stop_or_escalate_if: Direct API use is suspected and there is no clear recovery path from the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The OptiSweep software doesn't know what happened. It has no knowledge of the database of that transaction.

source_evidence.summary: Bypassing OptiSweep software and using external APIs directly can leave OptiSweep unaware of the transaction and create out-of-sync conditions.

steps.title: Do not perform direct API actions from this source
steps.purpose: Maintain the support boundary stated by the source and avoid unsupported actions.
steps.instruction: Stop short of performing direct RMS or external API actions based on this source alone. Treat this segment as a warning and interpretation boundary, not as authorization or a recovery method.
steps.expected_result: No unsupported direct API action is taken from this source guidance alone.
steps.healthy_condition: Support review stops at identification, interpretation, and escalation without bypassing OptiSweep.
steps.failure_condition: A direct API or RMS action is attempted based only on this source segment.

actions.label: Respect source boundary
actions.exact_action: Stop short of performing direct API actions from this source alone; this segment explicitly warns against bypassing the OptiSweep software flow.

stop_or_escalate_if: A direct API action is being considered without separate approved procedure.
System state is unclear after suspected bypass activity.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: You can't bypass our software or else we get out of sync...
If you have a problem and you want to bypass the OptiSweep service... the OptiSweep software doesn't know what happened.

success_criteria: Support determines whether direct RMS or external API activity may have bypassed OptiSweep.
The issue record documents the source-supported risk that OptiSweep may not know what happened.
No unsupported direct API action is taken from this source alone.
healthy_conditions: No direct API or RMS bypass activity is identified.
Reported actions remained within the normal OptiSweep software flow.
Support interpretation is documented without taking unsupported actions.
failure_conditions: Direct API or RMS bypass activity is suspected or confirmed.
OptiSweep may lack knowledge of the transaction.
System state is unclear and the source provides no recovery method.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if direct API use is suspected and system state is unclear.
Escalate rather than performing direct API actions from this source alone.
Note that the source does not provide a recovery method for resynchronizing systems after bypass activity.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: RMS overview and transcript context covering API communication and the warning against bypassing OptiSweep.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: You can't bypass our software or else we get out of sync...
If you have a problem and you want to bypass the OptiSweep service... the OptiSweep software doesn't know what happened. It has no knowledge of the database of that transaction.

missing_details: The source does not provide a recovery or resynchronization procedure after bypass activity.
The source does not define exact logging location or case documentation format for recording suspected bypass activity.
The source does not specify role boundaries beyond support-oriented interpretation.
The source does not provide commands, API endpoints, or approved direct-action procedures.
